cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the Colours section renders with Light selected by default and shows the "Customise light colours" link
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Customisation sidebar > Colours tab. The Colours section shows a Consent Template card at the top (with the law selector, defaulting to GDPR), followed by three colour scheme options: Light, Dark, and Auto-generated.

steps.expected: "Light", "Dark", and "Auto-generated" options should be visible. "Light" should be selected by default.
The law selector should show "GDPR" as the current selection by default.

steps.content: With "Light" selected, observe the area below the colour options.
steps.expected: A "Customise light colours" link should be visible below the colour options.

cases.rewrite_notes: C995 (display) and C998 (Light functionality) merged — C995 is a subset. Navigation steps 1–4 moved to preconditions. Sidebar tab list verification dropped as static presence check. Consent Template card position check dropped as alignment. Role variants (Account Owner, Admin, Editor) collapsed — identical behavior.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that selecting "Dark" updates the banner preview and shows the "Customise dark colours" link
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Customisation sidebar > Colours tab.

steps.expected: "Dark" is selected; the banner preview in the sidebar updates to display the dark colour scheme.

steps.content: Observe the area below the colour options.
steps.expected: A "Customise dark colours" link should appear in place of the "Customise light colours" link.

cases.rewrite_notes: Navigation steps dropped to preconditions. Static sidebar tab list check dropped. Role variants collapsed — identical behavior.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that selecting "Dark" on a Free plan shows the Custom CSS upgrade nudge
cases.preconditions: User is on a Free plan. User is on Cookie Banner > Customisation sidebar > Colours tab.

steps.expected: "Dark" is selected and the banner preview updates.

An in-app upgrade nudge for Custom CSS appears with a feature illustration, headline "Put your banner in the spotlight with custom CSS", a note that the feature is available in all premium plans, and two action buttons: "Try Pro for free" and "Dismiss".

cases.rewrite_notes: Steps 9–11 (verifying specific nudge headline text, image description, and button labels as copy checks) dropped. Step 12 ("Try it now" → upgrade page) extracted — already covered by Plan Gates C36866. This case covers only the nudge appearing.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that "Dismiss" closes the Custom CSS upgrade nudge on the Colours tab
cases.preconditions: User is on a Free plan. User is on Cookie Banner > Customisation sidebar > Colours tab. "Dark" is selected and the Custom CSS upgrade nudge is visible.

steps.content: Click the "Dismiss" button on the upgrade nudge.
steps.expected: The nudge closes. It should not reappear during the same browser session.

cases.rewrite_notes: Navigation and nudge appearance steps moved to preconditions. Admin/Editor role cases differ only in "Try it now" being disabled — that divergence is flagged as a permission case separately. Dismiss behavior is identical across all roles.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that selecting "Auto-generated" applies the website's colour scheme to the banner
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Customisation sidebar > Colours tab. The account's connected domain has been scanned and website colours are available (without scan data, selecting "Auto-generated" silently reverts to the Dark scheme).

steps.expected: "Auto-generated" is selected; the banner preview updates to display a colour scheme derived from the connected website's colours.

steps.content: Observe the area below the colour options.
steps.expected: A "Customise auto-generated colours" link should appear.

cases.rewrite_notes: Help icon tooltip step dropped (static copy). C16743 (Free plan Auto-generated upgrade nudge) not included here — already covered by Plan Gates C36864. Role variants collapsed — identical behavior.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that "Customise light colours" reveals per-component colour pickers under GDPR
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Customisation sidebar > Colours tab. Law is set to GDPR (default). "Light" is selected (default). The "Customise light colours" link is visible.

steps.expected: A "Custom" sub-option is activated. A "Customise colours" section appears with four accordion items: Cookie Notice, Preference Center, Revisit Consent Button, Alt Text for Blocked Content.

The following sub-options appear with their default light colours:
- Banner: Background #FFFFFF, Border #F4F4F4, Title #212121, Message #212121
- "Accept All" button: Background #1863DC, Border #1863DC, Text #FFFFFF
- "Reject All" button: Background #1863DC, Border #1863DC, Text #FFFFFF
- "Customise" button: Background TRANSPARENT, Border #1863DC, Text #1863DC

steps.content: Change one or more colour values under the Cookie Notice accordion.
steps.expected: The banner preview updates to reflect the changed colour on the corresponding element.

The following sub-options appear with their default colours:
- Toggle switch: Enabled state #1863DC, Disabled state #D0D5D2
- "Save My Preferences" button: Background #1863DC, Border #1863DC, Text #FFFFFF

steps.content: Change one or more colour values under the Preference Center accordion.
steps.expected: The banner preview updates accordingly.
"Floating button" sub-option appears with default colour: Background #0056A7.

steps.content: Expand the "Alt Text for Blocked Content" accordion.
steps.expected:
Sub-options appear with default colours:
- Background #000000
- Border #000000
- Text #FFFFFF
Changes are saved; the customised colours are applied to the live banner.

cases.rewrite_notes: Navigation and GDPR default verification steps moved to preconditions. Default hex colour values retained as behavioural spec (not dropped — they define product defaults). Duplicate assertions removed. Role variants collapsed — identical behavior.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that "Customise dark colours" reveals per-component colour pickers under GDPR
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Customisation sidebar > Colours tab. Law is set to GDPR (default). "Dark" is selected. The "Customise dark colours" link is visible.

steps.expected: A "Custom" sub-option is activated. A "Customise colours" section appears with four accordion items: Cookie Notice, Preference Center, Revisit Consent Button, Alt Text for Blocked Content.

The following sub-options appear with their default dark colours:
- Banner: Background #121212, Border #2A2A2A, Title #D0D0D0, Message #D0D0D0
- "Accept All" button: Background #1578F7, Border #1578F7, Text #FFFFFF
- "Reject All" button: Background #1578F7, Border #1578F7, Text #FFFFFF
- "Customise" button: Background TRANSPARENT, Border #D0D0D0, Text #D0D0D0

steps.content: Change one or more colour values under the Cookie Notice accordion.
steps.expected: The banner preview updates to reflect the change.

The following sub-options appear with their default colours:
- Toggle switch: Enabled state #1578F7, Disabled state #D0D5D2
- "Save My Preferences" button: Background #1578F7, Border #1578F7, Text #FFFFFF

steps.content: Change one or more colour values under the Preference Center accordion.
steps.expected: The banner preview updates accordingly.
"Floating button" sub-option appears with default colour: Background #0056A7.

steps.content: Expand the "Alt Text for Blocked Content" accordion.
steps.expected:
Sub-options appear with default colours:
- Background #000000
- Border #000000
- Text #FFFFFF
Changes are saved; the customised dark colours are applied to the live banner.

cases.rewrite_notes: Source step 9 has a typo ('when Light is selected') — corrected to 'when Dark is selected'. Duplicate step 15/16 in source (same Reject All/Customize dark colour assertion repeated) kept once. Role variants collapsed.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that "Customise light colours" reveals per-component colour pickers under US State Laws
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Customisation sidebar > Colours tab. Law selector is set to US State Laws. "Light" is selected (default). The "Customise light colours" link is visible.

steps.expected: A "Custom" sub-option is activated. A "Customise colours" section appears with four accordion items: Cookie Notice, Opt-out Center, Revisit Consent Button, Alt Text for Blocked Content.

The following sub-options appear with their default light colours:
- Banner: Background #FFFFFF, Border #F4F4F4, Title #212121, Message #212121
- "Do Not Sell" link: Text #1863DC

steps.content: Change one or more colour values under the Cookie Notice accordion.
steps.expected: The banner preview updates to reflect the change.

The following sub-options appear with their default colours:
- Checkbox: Enabled state #1863DC, Disabled state #FFFFFF
- "Cancel" button: Background #FFFFFF, Border #949494, Text #595959
- "Save My Preferences" button: Background #1863DC, Border #1863DC, Text #FFFFFF

steps.content: Change one or more colour values under the Opt-out Center accordion.
steps.expected: The banner preview updates accordingly.
"Floating button" sub-option appears with default colour: Background #0056A7.

steps.content: Expand the "Alt Text for Blocked Content" accordion.
steps.expected:
Sub-options appear with default colours:
- Background #000000
- Border #000000
- Text #FFFFFF
Changes are saved; the customised colours are applied to the live banner.

cases.rewrite_notes: Genuine variant from GDPR light case: Opt-out Center (Checkbox, Cancel, Save My Preferences) replaces Preference Center; Cookie Notice has 'Do Not Sell' link instead of 'Customise' button. Source step 17 mislabelled 'Toggle switch' but is under Opt-out Center — corrected to Checkbox. Role variants collapsed.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that "Customise dark colours" reveals per-component colour pickers under US State Laws
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Customisation sidebar > Colours tab. Law selector is set to US State Laws. "Dark" is selected. The "Customise dark colours" link is visible.

steps.expected: A "Custom" sub-option is activated. A "Customise colours" section appears with four accordion items: Cookie Notice, Opt-out Center, Revisit Consent Button, Alt Text for Blocked Content.

The following sub-options appear with their default dark colours:
- Banner: Background #121212, Border #2A2A2A, Title #D0D0D0, Message #D0D0D0
- "Do Not Sell" link: Text #609FFF

steps.content: Change one or more colour values under the Cookie Notice accordion.
steps.expected: The banner preview updates.

The following sub-options appear with their default dark colours:
- Checkbox: Enabled state #1578F7, Disabled state #FFFFFF
- "Cancel" button: Background TRANSPARENT, Border #949494, Text #A1A1A1
- "Save My Preferences" button: Background #1578F7, Border #1578F7, Text #FFFFFF

steps.content: Change one or more colour values under the Opt-out Center accordion.
steps.expected: The banner preview updates accordingly.
"Floating button" sub-option appears with default colour: Background #0056A7.

steps.content: Expand the "Alt Text for Blocked Content" accordion.
steps.expected:
Sub-options appear with default colours:
- Background #000000
- Border #000000
- Text #FFFFFF
Changes are saved; the customised dark colours are applied to the live banner.

cases.rewrite_notes: "Cancel" button dark variant differs from light (Background TRANSPARENT vs #FFFFFF). Role variants collapsed.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that "Customise auto-generated colours" allows overriding the auto-detected colour scheme
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Customisation sidebar > Colours tab. "Auto-generated" is selected. The "Customise auto-generated colours" link is visible.

steps.expected: A "Custom" sub-option is activated. A "Customise colours" section appears with the auto-detected colour values pre-populated for each banner element.

steps.content: Change one or more colour values in the Customise colours section.
steps.expected: The modified values are accepted; the banner preview updates to reflect the override.
Changes are saved; the overridden colours (not the auto-detected values) are applied to the live banner.

cases.rewrite_notes: No specific default hex values — colours are dynamically derived from the connected website. Role variants collapsed — identical behavior.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the Opt-out confirmation message sub-section in Opt-out Center shows correct default colours under US State Laws
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Customisation sidebar > Colours tab. Law selector is set to US State Laws. "Customise light colours" or "Customise dark colours" has been clicked and the Customise colours section is visible.

steps.expected: The accordion expands and shows four sub-sections: Checkbox, "Cancel" button, "Save My Preferences" button, and "Opt-out confirmation message".

steps.content: Scroll to the "Opt-out confirmation message" sub-section and observe its default colour values.
steps.expected:
The following default colours are shown:
- Background: #E5F4EF
- Icon: #00754E
- Text: #14142A
- Subtext: #4E4B66
These values are identical in both Light and Dark custom modes.

steps.content: Change one or more values under "Opt-out confirmation message".
steps.expected: The values are accepted. The banner preview updates to reflect the changed colour on the confirmation message element.

cases.rewrite_notes: Gap found during grill: the Opt-out Center accordion has a 4th sub-section (Opt-out confirmation message) not covered by any draft case. Colours are the same in Light and Dark custom modes.

cases.title: [Permissions] Verify that Admin and Editor cannot initiate the Custom CSS upgrade from the Colours tab nudge
cases.preconditions: User is logged in as Admin or Editor. User is on Cookie Banner > Customisation sidebar > Colours tab. "Dark" is selected and the Custom CSS upgrade nudge is visible.

steps.content: Observe the "Try it now" button on the upgrade nudge.
steps.expected: The "Try it now" button should be disabled (not clickable).

steps.content: Hover over the "Try it now" button.
steps.expected: A tooltip should appear: "Only account owner can change plan".

cases.rewrite_notes: Role divergence on billing-adjacent action (upgrade initiation). Account Owner can click Try it now; Admin and Editor cannot. Routes to section 14 (Permissions).
